# Icon System Playbook

## Core Rules

- One semantic meaning should map to one preferred icon.
- Prefer familiar metaphors for common actions.
- Use labels for ambiguous or high-risk actions.
- Keep icon styling consistent across the product.

## Sizing Rules

- Define a small, medium, and large icon scale.
- Align icons optically with adjacent text, not only by bounding box.
- Keep hit targets larger than icon glyph size for interactive controls.

## Usage Boundaries

- Navigation icons should support scanning, not replace labels when clarity suffers.
- Status icons should align with the product’s semantic color and message system.
- Decorative graphics should not compete with functional controls.

## Drift Signals

- Multiple similar icons are used for the same action.
- New screens import a different icon family by default.
- Icons appear in every control regardless of need.
- Alignment and optical weight vary across common components.
